NKJV.1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
NKJV.2: He was in the beginning with God.
NKJV.3: All things were made through Him, and without Him nothing was made that was made.
NKJV.4: In Him was life, and the life was the light of men.
NKJV.5: And the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not comprehend it.
NKJV.6: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
NKJV.7: This man came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all through him might believe.
NKJV.8: He was not that Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
NKJV.9: That was the true Light which gives light to every man coming into the world.
NKJV.10: He was in the world, and the world was made through Him, and the world did not know Him.
NKJV.11: He came to His own, and His own did not receive Him.
NKJV.12: But as many as received Him, to them He gave the right to become children of God, to those who believe in His name:
NKJV.13: who were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
NKJV.14: And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we beheld His glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth.
NKJV.15: John bore witness of Him and cried out, saying, “This was He of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me is preferred before me, for He was before me.’ ”
NKJV.16: And of His fullness we have all received, and grace for grace.
NKJV.17: For the law was given through Moses, but grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
NKJV.18: No one has seen God at any time. The only begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He has declared Him.
NKJV.19: Now this is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
NKJV.20: He confessed, and did not deny, but confessed, “I am not the Christ.”
NKJV.21: And they asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the Prophet?” And he answered, “No.”
NKJV.22: Then they said to him, “Who are you, that we may give an answer to those who sent us? What do you say about yourself?”
NKJV.23: He said: “I am ‘The voice of one crying in the wilderness: “Make straight the way of the LORD,” ’ as the prophet Isaiah said.”
NKJV.24: Now those who were sent were from the Pharisees.
NKJV.25: And they asked him, saying, “Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
NKJV.26: John answered them, saying, “I baptize with water, but there stands One among you whom you do not know.
NKJV.27: It is He who, coming after me, is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I am not worthy to loose.”
NKJV.28: These things were done in Bethabara beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
NKJV.29: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him, and said, “Behold! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
NKJV.30: This is He of whom I said, ‘After me comes a Man who is preferred before me, for He was before me.’
NKJV.31: I did not know Him; but that He should be revealed to Israel, therefore I came baptizing with water.”
NKJV.32: And John bore witness, saying, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and He remained upon Him.
NKJV.33: I did not know Him, but He who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘Upon whom you see the Spirit descending, and remaining on Him, this is He who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
NKJV.34: And I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God.”
NKJV.35: Again, the next day, John stood with two of his disciples.
NKJV.36: And looking at Jesus as He walked, he said, “Behold the Lamb of God!”
NKJV.37: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
NKJV.38: Then Jesus turned, and seeing them following, said to them, “What do you seek?” They said to Him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, when translated, Teacher), “where are You staying?”
NKJV.39: He said to them, “Come and see.” They came and saw where He was staying, and remained with Him that day (now it was about the tenth hour).
NKJV.40: One of the two who heard John speak, and followed Him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
NKJV.41: He first found his own brother Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated, the Christ).
NKJV.42: And he brought him to Jesus. Now when Jesus looked at him, He said, “You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas” (which is translated, A Stone).
NKJV.43: The following day Jesus wanted to go to Galilee, and He found Philip and said to him, “Follow Me.”
NKJV.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
NKJV.45: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found Him of whom Moses in the law, and also the prophets, wrote—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
NKJV.46: And Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
NKJV.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward Him, and said of him, “Behold,  an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!”
NKJV.48: Nathanael said to Him, “How do You know me?” Jesus answered and said to him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
NKJV.49: Nathanael answered and said to Him, “Rabbi, You are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”
NKJV.50: Jesus answered and said to him, “Because I said to you, ‘I saw you under the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these.”
NKJV.51: And He said to him, “Most assuredly, I say to you,  hereafter you shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.”

ESV.1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
ESV.2: He was in the beginning with God.
ESV.3: All things were made through him, and without him was not any thing made that was made.
ESV.4: In him was life,  and the life was the light of men.
ESV.5: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
ESV.6: There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
ESV.7: He came as a witness, to bear witness about the light, that all might believe through him.
ESV.8: He was not the light, but came to bear witness about the light.
ESV.9: The true light, which gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
ESV.10: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, yet the world did not know him.
ESV.11: He came to his own,  and his own people  did not receive him.
ESV.12: But to all who did receive him, who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God,
ESV.13: who were born, not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man, but of God.
ESV.14: And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we have seen his glory, glory as of the only Son  from the Father, full of grace and truth.
ESV.15: (John bore witness about him, and cried out, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me ranks before me, because he was before me.’”)
ESV.16: For from his fullness we have all received, grace upon grace.
ESV.17: For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
ESV.18: No one has ever seen God; the only God,  who is at the Father's side,  he has made him known.
ESV.19: And this is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
ESV.20: He confessed, and did not deny, but confessed, “I am not the Christ.”
ESV.21: And they asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the Prophet?” And he answered, “No.”
ESV.22: So they said to him, “Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
ESV.23: He said, “I am the voice of one crying out in the wilderness, ‘Make straight  the way of the Lord,’ as the prophet Isaiah said.”
ESV.24: (Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.)
ESV.25: They asked him, “Then why are you baptizing, if you are neither the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
ESV.26: John answered them, “I baptize with water, but among you stands one you do not know,
ESV.27: even he who comes after me, the strap of whose sandal I am not worthy to untie.”
ESV.28: These things took place in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
ESV.29: The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
ESV.30: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who ranks before me, because he was before me.’
ESV.31: I myself did not know him, but for this purpose I came baptizing with water, that he might be revealed to Israel.”
ESV.32: And John bore witness: “I saw the Spirit descend from heaven like a dove, and it remained on him.
ESV.33: I myself did not know him, but he who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘He on whom you see the Spirit descend and remain, this is he who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
ESV.34: And I have seen and have borne witness that this is the Son  of God.”
ESV.35: The next day again John was standing with two of his disciples,
ESV.36: and he looked at Jesus as he walked by and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”
ESV.37: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
ESV.38: Jesus turned and saw them following and said to them,  “What are you seeking?” And they said to him, “Rabbi” (which means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
ESV.39: He said to them, “Come and you will see.” So they came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day, for it was about the tenth hour.
ESV.40: One of the two who heard John speak and followed Jesus  was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.
ESV.41: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which means Christ).
ESV.42: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, “You are Simon the son of John. You shall be called Cephas” (which means Peter ).
ESV.43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
ESV.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
ESV.45: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found him of whom Moses in the Law and also the prophets wrote, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
ESV.46: Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
ESV.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said of him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom there is no deceit!”
ESV.48: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
ESV.49: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”
ESV.50: Jesus answered him, “Because I said to you, ‘I saw you under the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these.”
ESV.51: And he said to him, “Truly, truly, I say to you,  you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

NASB.1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
NASB.2: He was in the beginning with God.
NASB.3: All things came into being through Him, and apart from Him nothing came into being that has come into being.
NASB.4: In Him was life, and the life was the Light of men.
NASB.5: The Light shines in the darkness, and the darkness did not comprehend it.
NASB.6: There came a man sent from God, whose name was John.
NASB.7: He came as a witness, to testify about the Light, so that all might believe through him.
NASB.8: He was not the Light, but [he came] to testify about the Light.
NASB.9: There was the true Light which, coming into the world, enlightens every man.
NASB.10: He was in the world, and the world was made through Him, and the world did not know Him.
NASB.11: He came to His own, and those who were His own did not receive Him.
NASB.12: But as many as received Him, to them He gave the right to become children of God, [even] to those who believe in His name,
NASB.13: who were born, not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man, but of God.
NASB.14: And the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us, and we saw His glory, glory as of the only begotten from the Father, full of grace and truth.
NASB.15: John testified about Him and cried out, saying, 'This was He of whom I said, 'He who comes after me has a higher rank than I, for He existed before me.''
NASB.16: For of His fullness we have all received, and grace upon grace.
NASB.17: For the Law was given through Moses; grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.
NASB.18: No one has seen God at any time; the only begotten God who is in the bosom of the Father, He has explained [Him].
NASB.19: This is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent to him priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, 'Who are you?'
NASB.20: And he confessed and did not deny, but confessed, 'I am not the Christ.'
NASB.21: They asked him, 'What then? Are you Elijah?' And he said, 'I am not.' 'Are you the Prophet?' And he answered, 'No.'
NASB.22: Then they said to him, 'Who are you, so that we may give an answer to those who sent us? What do you say about yourself?'
NASB.23: He said, 'I am A VOICE OF ONE CRYING IN THE WILDERNESS, 'MAKE STRAIGHT THE WAY OF THE LORD,' as Isaiah the prophet said.'
NASB.24: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
NASB.25: They asked him, and said to him, 'Why then are you baptizing, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?'
NASB.26: John answered them saying, 'I baptize in water, [but] among you stands One whom you do not know.
NASB.27: '[It is] He who comes after me, the thong of whose sandal I am not worthy to untie.'
NASB.28: These things took place in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
NASB.29: The next day he saw Jesus coming to him and said, 'Behold, the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
NASB.30: 'This is He on behalf of whom I said, 'After me comes a Man who has a higher rank than I, for He existed before me.'
NASB.31: 'I did not recognize Him, but so that He might be manifested to Israel, I came baptizing in water.'
NASB.32: John testified saying, 'I have seen the Spirit descending as a dove out of heaven, and He remained upon Him.
NASB.33: 'I did not recognize Him, but He who sent me to baptize in water said to me, 'He upon whom you see the Spirit descending and remaining upon Him, this is the One who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.'
NASB.34: 'I myself have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.'
NASB.35: Again the next day John was standing with two of his disciples,
NASB.36: and he looked at Jesus as He walked, and said, 'Behold, the Lamb of God!'
NASB.37: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
NASB.38: And Jesus turned and saw them following, and said to them, 'What do you seek?' They said to Him, 'Rabbi (which translated means Teacher), where are You staying?'
NASB.39: He said to them, 'Come, and you will see.' So they came and saw where He was staying; and they stayed with Him that day, for it was about the tenth hour.
NASB.40: One of the two who heard John [speak] and followed Him, was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.
NASB.41: He found first his own brother Simon and said to him, 'We have found the Messiah ' (which translated means Christ).
NASB.42: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, 'You are Simon the son of John; you shall be called Cephas ' (which is translated Peter).
NASB.43: The next day He purposed to go into Galilee, and He found Philip. And Jesus said to him, 'Follow Me.'
NASB.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
NASB.45: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, 'We have found Him of whom Moses in the Law and [also] the Prophets wrote-- Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.'
NASB.46: Nathanael said to him, 'Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?' Philip said to him, 'Come and see.'
NASB.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to Him, and said of him, 'Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom there is no deceit!'
NASB.48: Nathanael said to Him, 'How do You know me?' Jesus answered and said to him, 'Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.'
NASB.49: Nathanael answered Him, 'Rabbi, You are the Son of God; You are the King of Israel.'
NASB.50: Jesus answered and said to him, 'Because I said to you that I saw you under the fig tree, do you believe? You will see greater things than these.'
NASB.51: And He said to him, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, you will see the heavens opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.'

NLT.1:
In the beginning the Word already existed.
The Word was with God,
and the Word was God.
NLT.2: He existed in the beginning with God.
NLT.3:
God created everything through him,
and nothing was created except through him.
NLT.4:
The Word gave life to everything that was created,
and his life brought light to everyone.
NLT.5:
The light shines in the darkness,
and the darkness can never extinguish it.
NLT.6: God sent a man, John the Baptist,
NLT.7: to tell about the light so that everyone might believe because of his testimony.
NLT.8: John himself was not the light; he was simply a witness to tell about the light.
NLT.9: The one who is the true light, who gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
NLT.10: He came into the very world he created, but the world didn’t recognize him.
NLT.11: He came to his own people, and even they rejected him.
NLT.12: But to all who believed him and accepted him, he gave the right to become children of God.
NLT.13: They are reborn — not with a physical birth resulting from human passion or plan, but a birth that comes from God.
NLT.14: So the Word became human and made his home among us. He was full of unfailing love and faithfulness. And we have seen his glory, the glory of the Father’s one and only Son.
NLT.15: John testified about him when he shouted to the crowds, “This is the one I was talking about when I said, ‘Someone is coming after me who is far greater than I am, for he existed long before me.’”
NLT.16: From his abundance we have all received one gracious blessing after another.
NLT.17: For the law was given through Moses, but God’s unfailing love and faithfulness came through Jesus Christ.
NLT.18: No one has ever seen God. But the unique One, who is himself God, is near to the Father’s heart. He has revealed God to us.
NLT.19: This was John’s testimony when the Jewish leaders sent priests and Temple assistants from Jerusalem to ask John, “Who are you?”
NLT.20: He came right out and said, “I am not the Messiah.”
NLT.21: “Well then, who are you?” they asked. “Are you Elijah?” “No,” he replied. “Are you the Prophet we are expecting?” “No.”
NLT.22: “Then who are you? We need an answer for those who sent us. What do you have to say about yourself?”
NLT.23:
John replied in the words of the prophet Isaiah: “I am a voice shouting in the wilderness,
‘Clear the way for the LORD’s coming!’”
NLT.24: Then the Pharisees who had been sent
NLT.25: asked him, “If you aren’t the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet, what right do you have to baptize?”
NLT.26: John told them, “I baptize with water, but right here in the crowd is someone you do not recognize.
NLT.27: Though his ministry follows mine, I’m not even worthy to be his slave and untie the straps of his sandal.”
NLT.28: This encounter took place in Bethany, an area east of the Jordan River, where John was baptizing.
NLT.29: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
NLT.30: He is the one I was talking about when I said, ‘A man is coming after me who is far greater than I am, for he existed long before me.’
NLT.31: I did not recognize him as the Messiah, but I have been baptizing with water so that he might be revealed to Israel.”
NLT.32: Then John testified, “I saw the Holy Spirit descending like a dove from heaven and resting upon him.
NLT.33: I didn’t know he was the one, but when God sent me to baptize with water, he told me, ‘The one on whom you see the Spirit descend and rest is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.’
NLT.34: I saw this happen to Jesus, so I testify that he is the Chosen One of God. ”
NLT.35: The following day John was again standing with two of his disciples.
NLT.36: As Jesus walked by, John looked at him and declared, “Look! There is the Lamb of God!”
NLT.37: When John’s two disciples heard this, they followed Jesus.
NLT.38: Jesus looked around and saw them following. “What do you want?” he asked them. They replied, “Rabbi” (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?”
NLT.39: “Come and see,” he said. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon when they went with him to the place where he was staying, and they remained with him the rest of the day.
NLT.40: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of these men who heard what John said and then followed Jesus.
NLT.41: Andrew went to find his brother, Simon, and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which means “Christ” ).
NLT.42: Then Andrew brought Simon to meet Jesus. Looking intently at Simon, Jesus said, “Your name is Simon, son of John — but you will be called Cephas” (which means “Peter” ).
NLT.43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Come, follow me.”
NLT.44: Philip was from Bethsaida, Andrew and Peter’s hometown.
NLT.45: Philip went to look for Nathanael and told him, “We have found the very person Moses and the prophets wrote about! His name is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
NLT.46: “Nazareth!” exclaimed Nathanael. “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” “Come and see for yourself,” Philip replied.
NLT.47: As they approached, Jesus said, “Now here is a genuine son of Israel — a man of complete integrity.”
NLT.48: “How do you know about me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus replied, “I could see you under the fig tree before Philip found you.”
NLT.49: Then Nathanael exclaimed, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God — the King of Israel!”
NLT.50: Jesus asked him, “Do you believe this just because I told you I had seen you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than this.”
NLT.51: Then he said, “I tell you the truth, you will all see heaven open and the angels of God going up and down on the Son of Man, the one who is the stairway between heaven and earth. ”

WEB.1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
WEB.2: The same was in the beginning with God.
WEB.3: All things were made through him. Without him was not anything made that has been made.
WEB.4: In him was life, and the life was the light of men.
WEB.5: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome  it.
WEB.6: There came a man, sent from God, whose name was John.
WEB.7: The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him.
WEB.8: He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light.
WEB.9: The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world.
WEB.10: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him.
WEB.11: He came to his own, and those who were his own didn’t receive him.
WEB.12: But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name:
WEB.13: who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
WEB.14: The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.
WEB.15: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’”
WEB.16: From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.
WEB.17: For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.
WEB.18: No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son,  who is in the bosom of the Father, he has declared him.
WEB.19: This is John’s testimony, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
WEB.20: He declared, and didn’t deny, but he declared, “I am not the Christ.”
WEB.21: They asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the prophet?” He answered, “No.”
WEB.22: They said therefore to him, “Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
WEB.23: He said, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’  as Isaiah the prophet said.”
WEB.24: The ones who had been sent were from the Pharisees.
WEB.25: They asked him, “Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”
WEB.26: John answered them, “I baptize in water, but among you stands one whom you don’t know.
WEB.27: He is the one who comes after me, who is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to loosen.”
WEB.28: These things were done in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
WEB.29: The next day, he saw Jesus coming to him, and said, “Behold,  the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
WEB.30: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who is preferred before me, for he was before me.’
WEB.31: I didn’t know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water: that he would be revealed to Israel.”
WEB.32: John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.
WEB.33: I didn’t recognize him, but he who sent me to baptize in water, he said to me, ‘On whomever you will see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.’
WEB.34: I have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.”
WEB.35: Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples,
WEB.36: and he looked at Jesus as he walked, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”
WEB.37: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
WEB.38: Jesus turned, and saw them following, and said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), “where are you staying?”
WEB.39: He said to them, “Come, and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.
WEB.40: One of the two who heard John, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
WEB.41: He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is, being interpreted, Christ ).
WEB.42: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him, and said, “You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas” (which is by interpretation, Peter).
WEB.43: On the next day, he was determined to go out into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
WEB.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
WEB.45: Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, “We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
WEB.46: Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
WEB.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and said about him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!”
WEB.48: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
WEB.49: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!”
WEB.50: Jesus answered him, “Because I told you, ‘I saw you underneath the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these!”
WEB.51: He said to him, “Most certainly, I tell you, hereafter you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

CSB17.1: In the beginning <sup>ⓒ</sup> was the Word, <sup>ⓓ</sup> and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.2: He was with God in the beginning. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.3: All things were created <sup>ⓖ</sup> through him, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.
CSB17.4: In him was life, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> and that life was the light <sup>ⓙ</sup> of men.
CSB17.5: That light shines <sup>ⓚ</sup> in the darkness, and yet the darkness did not overcome <sup>[2]</sup> it. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.6: There was a man sent from God whose name was John. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.7: He came as a witness to testify about the light, so that all might believe through him. <sup>[3]</sup>
CSB17.8: He was not the light, but he came to testify <sup>ⓝ</sup> about the light.
CSB17.9: The true light <sup>ⓐ</sup> that gives light to everyone, was coming into the world. <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.10: He was in the world, and the world was created <sup>ⓒ</sup> through him, and yet the world did not recognize him.
CSB17.11: He came to his own, and his own people did not receive him.
CSB17.12: But to all who did receive <sup>ⓓ</sup> him, <sup>ⓔ</sup> he gave them the right <sup>ⓕ</sup> to be <sup>[5]</sup> children <sup>ⓖ</sup> of God, <sup>ⓗ</sup> to those who believe <sup>ⓘ</sup> in his name, <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.13: who were born, <sup>ⓚ</sup> not of natural descent, <sup>[6]</sup> or of the will <sup>ⓛ</sup> of the flesh, <sup>ⓜ</sup> or of the will of man, <sup>[7]</sup> but of God. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.14: The Word <sup>ⓐ</sup> became flesh <sup>ⓑ</sup> and dwelt <sup>[8]</sup> among us. We observed his glory, <sup>ⓒ</sup> the glory as the one and only <sup>ⓓ</sup> Son <sup>[9]</sup> from the Father, full of grace and truth.
CSB17.15: (John testified concerning him and exclaimed, “This was the one of whom I said, ‘The one coming after me <sup>ⓔ</sup> ranks ahead of me, <sup>ⓕ</sup> because he existed before me.’”) <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.16: Indeed, we have all received grace <sup>ⓗ</sup> upon <sup>[10]</sup> grace from his fullness,
CSB17.17: for the law was given through Moses; <sup>ⓘ</sup> grace and truth <sup>ⓙ</sup> came through Jesus Christ.
CSB17.18: No one has ever seen God. The one and only <sup>ⓚ</sup> Son, who is himself God and <sup>[11]</sup> is at the Father’s <sup>ⓛ</sup> side —he has revealed him. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.19: This was John’s testimony when the Jews from Jerusalem <sup>ⓝ</sup> sent priests <sup>ⓐ</sup> and Levites <sup>ⓑ</sup> to ask him, “Who are you?”
CSB17.20: He didn’t deny it but confessed: “I am not the Messiah.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.21: “What then?” they asked him. “Are you Elijah?” <sup>ⓓ</sup> “I am not,” he said. “Are you the Prophet?” <sup>ⓔ</sup> “No,” he answered.
CSB17.22: “Who are you, then?” they asked. “We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What can you tell us about yourself?”
CSB17.23: He said, “I am a voice of one crying out in the wilderness: Make straight the way of the Lord [12] ⓕ  — just as Isaiah ⓖ the prophet said.”
CSB17.24: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.25: So they asked him, “Why then do you baptize if you aren’t the Messiah, or Elijah, or the Prophet?”
CSB17.26: “I baptize with <sup>[13]</sup> water,” <sup>ⓘ</sup> John answered them. “Someone stands among you, but you don’t know him.
CSB17.27: He is the one coming after me, <sup>[14]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to untie.”
CSB17.28: All this happened in Bethany <sup>[15]</sup> across the Jordan, <sup>ⓚ</sup> where John was baptizing.
CSB17.29: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Here is the Lamb of God, <sup>ⓛ</sup> who takes away the sin <sup>ⓜ</sup> of the world!
CSB17.30: This is the one I told you about: ‘After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me, because he existed before me.’
CSB17.31: I didn’t know him, <sup>ⓝ</sup> but I came baptizing with water so he might be revealed <sup>ⓐ</sup> to Israel.”
CSB17.32: And John testified, “I saw the Spirit <sup>ⓑ</sup> descending from heaven like a dove, and he rested on him. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.33: I didn’t know him, but he who sent me to baptize with water told me, ‘The one you see the Spirit descending and resting on — he is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’ <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.34: I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God.” <sup>[16]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.35: The next day, John was standing with two of his disciples.
CSB17.36: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, “Look, the Lamb of God!”
CSB17.37: The two disciples heard him say this and followed Jesus.
CSB17.38: When Jesus turned and noticed them following him, he asked them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” <sup>ⓕ</sup> (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?”
CSB17.39: “Come and you’ll see,” he replied. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about four in the afternoon. <sup>[17]</sup>
CSB17.40: Andrew, <sup>ⓖ</sup> Simon Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard John and followed him.
CSB17.41: He first found his own brother Simon and told him, “We have found the Messiah” <sup>[18]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup> (which is translated “the Christ”), <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.42: and he brought Simon to Jesus. When Jesus saw him, he said, “You are Simon, son of John. <sup>[19]</sup>You will be called Cephas” <sup>ⓙ</sup> (which is translated “Peter” <sup>[20]</sup>).
CSB17.43: The next day Jesus <sup>[21]</sup> decided to leave for Galilee. He found Philip <sup>ⓚ</sup> and told him, “Follow me.”
CSB17.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, <sup>ⓛ</sup> the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
CSB17.45: Philip found Nathanael <sup>ⓜ</sup> and told him, “We have found the one Moses wrote about in the law (and so did the prophets <sup>ⓝ</sup>): Jesus the son of Joseph, <sup>ⓐ</sup> from Nazareth.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.46: “Can anything good <sup>ⓒ</sup> come out of Nazareth?” Nathanael asked him. “Come and see,” Philip answered.
CSB17.47: Then Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, “Here truly is an Israelite <sup>ⓓ</sup> in whom there is no deceit.”
CSB17.48: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked.  “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you,” Jesus answered.
CSB17.49: “Rabbi,” <sup>ⓔ</sup> Nathanael replied, “You are the Son <sup>ⓕ</sup> of God; you are the King of Israel!” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.50: Jesus responded to him, “Do you believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than this.”
CSB17.51: Then he said, “Truly I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God <sup>ⓗ</sup> ascending and descending <sup>ⓘ</sup> on the Son of Man.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>

AMP.1: In the beginning [before all time] was the Word ( Christ), and the Word was with God, and the Word was God Himself.
AMP.2: He was [continually existing] in the beginning [co-eternally] with God.
AMP.3: All things were made and came into existence through Him; and without Him not even one thing was made that has come into being.
AMP.4: In Him was life [and the power to bestow life], and the life was the Light of men.
AMP.5: The Light shines on in the darkness, and the darkness did not understand it or overpower it or appropriate it or absorb it [and is unreceptive to it].
AMP.6: There came a man commissioned and sent from God, whose name was John.
AMP.7: This man came as a witness, to testify about the Light, so that all might believe [in Christ, the Light] through him.
AMP.8: John was not the Light, but came to testify about the Light.
AMP.9: There it was—the true Light [the genuine, perfect, steadfast Light] which, coming into the world, enlightens everyone.
AMP.10: He (Christ) was in the world, and though the world was made through Him, the world did not recognize Him.
AMP.11: He came to that which was His own [that which belonged to Him—His world, His creation, His possession], and those who were His own [people—the Jewish nation] did not receive and welcome Him.
AMP.12: But to as many as did receive and welcome Him, He gave the right [the authority, the privilege] to become children of God, that is, to those who believe in (adhere to, trust in, and rely on) His name—
AMP.13: who were born, not of blood [natural conception], nor of the will of the flesh [physical impulse], nor of the will of man [that of a natural father], but of God [that is, a divine and supernatural birth—they are born of God—spiritually transformed, renewed, sanctified].
AMP.14: And the Word (Christ) became flesh, and lived among us; and we [actually] saw His glory, glory as belongs to the [One and] only begotten Son of the Father, [the Son who is truly unique, the only One of His kind, who is] full of grace and truth (absolutely free of deception).
AMP.15: John testified [repeatedly] about Him and has cried out [testifying officially for the record, with validity and relevance], "This was He of whom I said, 'He who comes after me has a higher rank than I and has priority over me, for He existed before me.'"
AMP.16: For out of His fullness [the superabundance of His grace and truth] we have all received grace upon grace [spiritual blessing upon spiritual blessing, favor upon favor, and gift heaped upon gift].
AMP.17: For the Law was given through Moses, but grace [the unearned, undeserved favor of God] and truth came through Jesus Christ.
AMP.18: No one has seen God [His essence, His divine nature] at any time; the [One and] only begotten God [that is, the unique Son] who is in the intimate presence of the Father, He has explained Him [and interpreted and revealed the awesome wonder of the Father].
AMP.19: This is the testimony of John [the Baptist] when the Jews sent priests and Levites to him from Jerusalem to ask him, "Who are you?"
AMP.20: And he confessed [truthfully] and did not deny [that he was only a man], but acknowledged, "I am not the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed)!"
AMP.21: They asked him, "What then? Are you Elijah?" And he said, "I am not." "Are you the [promised] Prophet?" And he answered, "No."
AMP.22: Then they said to him, "Who are you? Tell us, so that we may give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?"
AMP.23: He said, "I am <e>the voice of one shouting in the wilderness, 'Make straight the way of the Lord</e>,' as the prophet Isaiah said."
AMP.24: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
AMP.25: They asked him, "Why then are you baptizing, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?"
AMP.26: John answered them, "I baptize [only] in water, but among you there stands One whom you do not recognize and of whom you know nothing.
AMP.27: It is He [the preeminent One] who comes after me, the strap of whose sandal I am not worthy to untie [even as His slave]."
AMP.28: These things occurred in Bethany across the Jordan [at the Jordan River crossing], where John was baptizing.
AMP.29: The next day he saw Jesus coming to him and said, "Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
AMP.30: This is He on behalf of whom I said, 'After me comes a Man who has a higher rank than I and has priority over me, for He existed before me.'
AMP.31: I did not recognize Him [as the Messiah]; but I came baptizing in water so that He would be [publicly] revealed to Israel."
AMP.32: John gave [further] evidence [testifying officially for the record, with validity and relevance], saying, "I have seen the Spirit descending as a dove out of heaven, and He remained upon Him.
AMP.33: I did not recognize Him [as the Messiah], but He who sent me to baptize in water said to me, 'He upon whom you see the Spirit descend and remain, this One is He who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.'
AMP.34: I myself have [actually] seen [that happen], and my testimony is that this is the Son of God!"
AMP.35: Again the next day John was standing with two of his disciples,
AMP.36: and he looked at Jesus as He walked along, and said, "Look! The Lamb of God!"
AMP.37: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
AMP.38: And Jesus turned and saw them following Him, and asked them, "What do you want?" They answered Him, "Rabbi (which translated means Teacher), where are You staying?"
AMP.39: He said to them, "Come, and you will see." So they went [with Him] and saw where He was staying; and they stayed with Him that day, for it was about the tenth hour.
AMP.40: One of the two who heard what John said and [as a result] followed Jesus was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.
AMP.41: He first looked for and found his own brother Simon and told him, "We have found the Messiah" (which translated means the Christ).
AMP.42: Andrew brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, "You are Simon the son of John. You shall be called Cephas (which is translated Peter)."
AMP.43: The next day Jesus decided to go into Galilee, and He found Philip and said to him, "Follow Me [as My disciple, accepting Me as your Master and Teacher, and walking the same path of life that I walk]."
AMP.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
AMP.45: Philip found Nathanael and told him, "We have found the One Moses in the Law and also the Prophets wrote about—Jesus from Nazareth, the son of Joseph [according to public record]."
AMP.46: Nathanael answered him, "Can anything good come out of Nazareth?" Philip replied, "Come and see."
AMP.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward Him, and said of him, "Here is an Israelite indeed [a true descendant of Jacob], in whom there is no guile nor deceit nor duplicity!"
AMP.48: Nathanael said to Jesus, "How do You know [these things about] me?" Jesus answered, "Before Philip called you, when you were still under the fig tree, I saw you."
AMP.49: Nathanael answered, "Rabbi (Teacher), You are the Son of God; You are the King of Israel."
AMP.50: Jesus replied, "Because I said to you that I saw you under the fig tree, do you believe [in Me]? You will see greater things than this."
AMP.51: Then He said to him, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man [the bridge between heaven and earth]."

MSG.1: The Word was first, the Word present to God, God present to the Word. The Word was God,
MSG.2: in readiness for God from day one.
MSG.3: Everything was created through him; nothing—not one thing!— came into being without him.
MSG.4: What came into existence was Life, and the Life was Light to live by.
MSG.5: The Life-Light blazed out of the darkness; the darkness couldn't put it out.
MSG.6: There once was a man, his name John, sent by God
MSG.7: to point out the way to the Life-Light. He came to show everyone where to look, who to believe in.
MSG.8: John was not himself the Light; he was there to show the way to the Light.
MSG.9: The Life-Light was the real thing: Every person entering Life he brings into Light.
MSG.10: He was in the world, the world was there through him, and yet the world didn't even notice.
MSG.11: He came to his own people, but they didn't want him.
MSG.12: But whoever did want him, who believed he was who he claimed and would do what he said, He made to be their true selves, their child-of-God selves.
MSG.13: These are the God-begotten, not blood-begotten, not flesh-begotten, not sex-begotten.
MSG.14: The Word became flesh and blood, and moved into the neighborhood. We saw the glory with our own eyes, the one-of-a-kind glory, like Father, like Son, Generous inside and out, true from start to finish.
MSG.15: John pointed him out and called, "This is the One! The One I told you was coming after me but in fact was ahead of me. He has always been ahead of me, has always had the first word."
MSG.16: We all live off his generous bounty, gift after gift after gift.
MSG.17: We got the basics from Moses, and then this exuberant giving and receiving, This endless knowing and understanding— all this came through Jesus, the Messiah.
MSG.18: No one has ever seen God, not so much as a glimpse. This one-of-a-kind God-Expression, who exists at the very heart of the Father, has made him plain as day.
MSG.19: When Jews from Jerusalem sent a group of priests and officials to ask John who he was, he was completely honest.
MSG.20: He didn't evade the question. He told the plain truth: "I am not the Messiah."
MSG.21: They pressed him, "Who, then? Elijah?" "I am not." "The Prophet?" "No."
MSG.22: Exasperated, they said, "Who, then? We need an answer for those who sent us. Tell us something—anything!—about yourself."
MSG.23: "I'm thunder in the desert: 'Make the road straight for God!' I'm doing what the prophet Isaiah preached."
MSG.24: Those sent to question him were from the Pharisee party.
MSG.25: Now they had a question of their own: "If you're neither the Messiah, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet, why do you baptize?"
MSG.26: John answered, "I only baptize using water. A person you don't recognize has taken his stand in your midst.
MSG.27: He comes after me, but he is not in second place to me. I'm not even worthy to hold his coat for him."
MSG.28: These conversations took place in Bethany on the other side of the Jordan, where John was baptizing at the time.
MSG.29: The very next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and yelled out,
MSG.30: "Here he is, God's Passover Lamb! He forgives the sins of the world! This is the man I've been talking about, 'the One who comes after me but is really ahead of me.'
MSG.31: I knew nothing about who he was—only this: that my task has been to get Israel ready to recognize him as the God-Revealer. That is why I came here baptizing with water, giving you a good bath and scrubbing sins from your life so you can get a fresh start with God."
MSG.32: John clinched his witness with this: "I watched the Spirit, like a dove flying down out of the sky, making himself at home in him.
MSG.33: I repeat, I know nothing about him except this: The One who authorized me to baptize with water told me, 'The One on whom you see the Spirit come down and stay, this One will baptize with the Holy Spirit.'
MSG.34: That's exactly what I saw happen, and I'm telling you, there's no question about it: This is the Son of God."
MSG.35: The next day John was back at his post with two disciples, who were watching.
MSG.36: He looked up, saw Jesus walking nearby, and said, "Here he is, God's Passover Lamb."
MSG.37: The two disciples heard him and went after Jesus.
MSG.38: Jesus looked over his shoulder and said to them, "What are you after?" They said, "Rabbi" (which means "Teacher"), "where are you staying?"
MSG.39: He replied, "Come along and see for yourself." They came, saw where he was living, and ended up staying with him for the day. It was late afternoon when this happened.
MSG.40: Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, was one of the two who heard John's witness and followed Jesus.
MSG.41: The first thing he did after finding where Jesus lived was find his own brother, Simon, telling him, "We've found the Messiah" (that is, "Christ").
MSG.42: He immediately led him to Jesus. Jesus took one look up and said, "You're John's son, Simon? From now on your name is Cephas" (or Peter, which means "Rock").
MSG.43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. When he got there, he ran across Philip and said, "Come, follow me."
MSG.44: (Philip's hometown was Bethsaida, the same as Andrew and Peter.)
MSG.45: Philip went and found Nathanael and told him, "We've found the One Moses wrote of in the Law, the One preached by the prophets. It's Jesus, Joseph's son, the one from Nazareth!"
MSG.46: Nathanael said, "Nazareth? You've got to be kidding." But Philip said, "Come, see for yourself."
MSG.47: When Jesus saw him coming he said, "There's a real Israelite, not a false bone in his body."
MSG.48: Nathanael said, "Where did you get that idea? You don't know me." Jesus answered, "One day, long before Philip called you here, I saw you under the fig tree."
MSG.49: Nathanael exclaimed, "Rabbi! You are the Son of God, the King of Israel!"
MSG.50: Jesus said, "You've become a believer simply because I say I saw you one day sitting under the fig tree? You haven't seen anything yet!
MSG.51: Before this is over you're going to see heaven open and God's angels descending to the Son of Man and ascending again."

NIV.1:
The Word Became Flesh
In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
NIV.2: He was with God in the beginning.
NIV.3: Through him all things were made; without him nothing was made that has been made.
NIV.4: In him was life, and that life was the light of men.
NIV.5: The light shines in the darkness, but the darkness has not understood it.
NIV.6: There came a man who was sent from God; his name was John.
NIV.7: He came as a witness to testify concerning that light, so that through him all men might believe.
NIV.8: He himself was not the light; he came only as a witness to the light.
NIV.9: The true light that gives light to every man was coming into the world.
NIV.10: He was in the world, and though the world was made through him, the world did not recognize him.
NIV.11: He came to that which was his own, but his own did not receive him.
NIV.12: Yet to all who received him, to those who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God—
NIV.13: children born not of natural descent, nor of human decision or a husband’s will, but born of God.
NIV.14: The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us. We have seen his glory, the glory of the One and Only, who came from the Father, full of grace and truth.
NIV.15: John testifies concerning him. He cries out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me.’”
NIV.16: From the fullness of his grace we have all received one blessing after another.
NIV.17: For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
NIV.18: No one has ever seen God, but God the One and Only, who is at the Father’s side, has made him known.
NIV.19:
John the Baptist Denies Being the Christ
Now this was John’s testimony when the Jews of Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him who he was.
NIV.20: He did not fail to confess, but confessed freely, “I am not the Christ.”
NIV.21:
They asked him, “Then who are you? Are you Elijah?”
He said, “I am not.”
“Are you the Prophet?”
He answered, “No.”
NIV.22: Finally they said, “Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
NIV.23: John replied in the words of Isaiah the prophet, “I am the voice of one calling in the desert, ‘Make straight the way for the Lord.’”
NIV.24: Now some Pharisees who had been sent
NIV.25: questioned him, “Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
NIV.26: “I baptize with water,” John replied, “but among you stands one you do not know.
NIV.27: He is the one who comes after me, the thongs of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.”
NIV.28: This all happened at Bethany on the other side of the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
NIV.29:
Jesus the Lamb of God
The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
NIV.30: This is the one I meant when I said, ‘A man who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me.’
NIV.31: I myself did not know him, but the reason I came baptizing with water was that he might be revealed to Israel.”
NIV.32: Then John gave this testimony: “I saw the Spirit come down from heaven as a dove and remain on him.
NIV.33: I would not have known him, except that the one who sent me to baptize with water told me, ‘The man on whom you see the Spirit come down and remain is he who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.’
NIV.34: I have seen and I testify that this is the Son of God.”
NIV.35:
Jesus’ First Disciples
The next day John was there again with two of his disciples.
NIV.36: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, “Look, the Lamb of God!”
NIV.37: When the two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.
NIV.38:
Turning around, Jesus saw them following and asked, “What do you want?”
They said, “Rabbi” (which means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
NIV.39:
“Come,” he replied, “and you will see.”
So they went and saw where he was staying, and spent that day with him. It was about the tenth hour.
NIV.40: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard what John had said and who had followed Jesus.
NIV.41: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother Simon and tell him, “We have found the Messiah” (that is, the Christ).
NIV.42:
And he brought him to Jesus.
Jesus looked at him and said, “You are Simon son of John. You will be called Cephas” (which, when translated, is Peter).
NIV.43:
Jesus Calls Philip and Nathanael
The next day Jesus decided to leave for Galilee. Finding Philip, he said to him, “Follow me.”
NIV.44: Philip, like Andrew and Peter, was from the town of Bethsaida.
NIV.45: Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law, and about whom the prophets also wrote—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
NIV.46:
“Nazareth! Can anything good come from there?” Nathanael asked.
“Come and see,” said Philip.
NIV.47: When Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, he said of him, “Here is a true Israelite, in whom there is nothing false.”
NIV.48:
“How do you know me?” Nathanael asked.
Jesus answered, “I saw you while you were still under the fig tree before Philip called you.”
NIV.49: Then Nathanael declared, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel.”
NIV.50: Jesus said, “You believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree. You shall see greater things than that.”
NIV.51: He then added, “I tell you the truth, you shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

NRSVCE.17: The law indeed was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.18: No one has ever seen God. It is God the only Son, who is close to the Father’s heart, who has made him known.
NRSVCE.19: This is the testimony given by John when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
NRSVCE.20: He confessed and did not deny it, but confessed, “I am not the Messiah.”
NRSVCE.21: And they asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you the prophet?” He answered, “No.”
NRSVCE.22: Then they said to him, “Who are you? Let us have an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
NRSVCE.23:
He said, “I am the voice of one crying out in the wilderness,
‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’”
as the prophet Isaiah said.
NRSVCE.24: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.
NRSVCE.25: They asked him, “Why then are you baptizing if you are neither the Messiah, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”
NRSVCE.26: John answered them, “I baptize with water. Among you stands one whom you do not know,
NRSVCE.27: the one who is coming after me; I am not worthy to untie the thong of his sandal.”
NRSVCE.28: This took place in Bethany across the Jordan where John was baptizing.
NRSVCE.29: The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him and declared, “Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
NRSVCE.30: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me because he was before me.’
NRSVCE.31: I myself did not know him; but I came baptizing with water for this reason, that he might be revealed to Israel.”
NRSVCE.32: And John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it remained on him.
NRSVCE.33: I myself did not know him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘He on whom you see the Spirit descend and remain is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
NRSVCE.34: And I myself have seen and have testified that this is the Son of God.”
NRSVCE.35: The next day John again was standing with two of his disciples,
NRSVCE.36: and as he watched Jesus walk by, he exclaimed, “Look, here is the Lamb of God!”
NRSVCE.37: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
NRSVCE.38: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he said to them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which translated means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
NRSVCE.39: He said to them, “Come and see.” They came and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four o’clock in the afternoon.
NRSVCE.40: One of the two who heard John speak and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
NRSVCE.41: He first found his brother Simon and said to him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated Anointed ).
NRSVCE.42: He brought Simon to Jesus, who looked at him and said, “You are Simon son of John. You are to be called Cephas” (which is translated Peter ).
NRSVCE.43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
NRSVCE.44: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
NRSVCE.45: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, “We have found him about whom Moses in the law and also the prophets wrote, Jesus son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
NRSVCE.46: Nathanael said to him, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
NRSVCE.47: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said of him, “Here is truly an Israelite in whom there is no deceit!”
NRSVCE.48: Nathanael asked him, “Where did you get to know me?” Jesus answered, “I saw you under the fig tree before Philip called you.”
NRSVCE.49: Nathanael replied, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”
NRSVCE.50: Jesus answered, “Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these.”
NRSVCE.51: And he said to him, “Very truly, I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of Man.”

NET.1: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was fully God.
NET.2: The Word was with God in the beginning.
NET.3: All things were created by him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.
NET.4: In him was life, and the life was the light of mankind.
NET.5: And the light shines on in the darkness, but the darkness has not mastered it.
NET.6: A man came, sent from God, whose name was John.
NET.7: He came as a witness to testify about the light, so that everyone might believe through him.
NET.8: He himself was not the light, but he came to testify about the light.
NET.9: The true light, who gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
NET.10: He was in the world, and the world was created by him, but the world did not recognize him.
NET.11: He came to what was his own, but his own people did not receive him.
NET.12: But to all who have received him — those who believe in his name — he has given the right to become God’s children
NET.13: — children not born by human parents or by human desire or a husband’s decision, but by God.
NET.14: Now the Word became flesh and took up residence among us. We saw his glory — the glory of the one and only, full of grace and truth, who came from the Father.
NET.15: John testified about him and shouted out, “This one was the one about whom I said, ‘He who comes after me is greater than I am, because he existed before me.’”
NET.16: For we have all received from his fullness one gracious gift after another.
NET.17: For the law was given through Moses, but grace and truth came about through Jesus Christ.
NET.18: No one has ever seen God. The only one, himself God, who is in closest fellowship with the Father, has made God known.
NET.19: Now this was John’s testimony when the Jewish leaders sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”
NET.20: He confessed — he did not deny but confessed — “I am not the Christ!”
NET.21: So they asked him, “Then who are you? Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not!” “Are you the Prophet?” He answered, “No!”
NET.22: Then they said to him, “Who are you? Tell us so that we can give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
NET.23: John said, “I am the voice of one shouting in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way for the Lord,’ as Isaiah the prophet said.”
NET.24: (Now they had been sent from the Pharisees. )
NET.25: So they asked John, “Why then are you baptizing if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
NET.26: John answered them, “I baptize with water. Among you stands one whom you do not recognize,
NET.27: who is coming after me. I am not worthy to untie the strap of his sandal!”
NET.28: These things happened in Bethany across the Jordan River where John was baptizing.
NET.29: On the next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Look, the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
NET.30: This is the one about whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who is greater than I am, because he existed before me.’
NET.31: I did not recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he could be revealed to Israel.”
NET.32: Then John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending like a dove from heaven, and it remained on him.
NET.33: And I did not recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, ‘The one on whom you see the Spirit descending and remaining — this is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
NET.34: I have both seen and testified that this man is the Chosen One of God.”
NET.35: Again the next day John was standing there with two of his disciples.
NET.36: Gazing at Jesus as he walked by, he said, “Look, the Lamb of God!”
NET.37: When John’s two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.
NET.38: Jesus turned around and saw them following and said to them, “What do you want?” So they said to him, “Rabbi” (which is translated Teacher), “where are you staying?”
NET.39: Jesus answered, “Come and you will see.” So they came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. Now it was about four o’clock in the afternoon.
NET.40: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus.
NET.41: He first found his own brother Simon and told him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is translated Christ).
NET.42: Andrew brought Simon to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, “You are Simon, the son of John. You will be called Cephas” (which is translated Peter).
NET.43: On the next day Jesus wanted to set out for Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
NET.44: (Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the town of Andrew and Peter.)
NET.45: Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the one Moses wrote about in the law, and the prophets also wrote about — Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”
NET.46: Nathanael replied, “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip replied, “Come and see.”
NET.47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and exclaimed, “Look, a true Israelite in whom there is no deceit!”
NET.48: Nathanael asked him, “How do you know me?” Jesus replied, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
NET.49: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the king of Israel!”
NET.50: Jesus said to him, “Because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree, do you believe? You will see greater things than these.”
NET.51: He continued, “I tell all of you the solemn truth — you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”
